'''Innes Senior''' (born 30 May 2000) is an {{rlnt|Ireland}} international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. He has spent time on loan at [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the Super League and at [[Workington Town]] in [[RFL League 1|League 1]].

==Background==
Senior was born in [[Huddersfield]], West Yorkshire, England.

Along with his twin brother [[Louis Senior|Louis]], Innes is a product of the [[Huddersfield Giants|Giants]]' academy system and both made their first team debuts over Easter 2018.<ref>{{cite news| url = https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants/early-seniors-service-for-huddersfield-giants-comes-as-welcome-surprise-1-9106960| title = Early Seniors' service for Huddersfield Giants comes as welcome surprise |newspaper= Yorkshire Post|accessdate=27 May 2019}}</ref>

==Career==
===Huddersfield Giants===
In 2018, he made his [[Super League]] début for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] against the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league|title=Catalans Dragons v Huddersfield Giants|publisher=BBC|work=BBC Sport|access-date=2 April 2018}}</ref> His twin brother [[Louis Senior|Louis]] had made his début in the previous game against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].

Senior became the first player born in or after the year 2000 to score in the Super League when he scored a try for the Huddersfield club against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in April 2018.

On 28 May 2022, Senior played for Huddersfield in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] loss to [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web |date=26 May 2022 |title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/ |accessdate=28 May 2022 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

Senior was limited to only six games with Huddersfield in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished ninth on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web |date=29 September 2023 |title=Ian Watson explains why Huddersfield Giants underperformed in Super League 2023 |url=https://www.totalrl.com/ian-watson-explains-why-huddersfield-giants-underperformed-in-super-league-2023/#:~:text=HUDDERSFIELD%20GIANTS%20have%20been%20one,silverware%20once%20more%20this%20season./ |website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>

==== Wakefield Trinity (loan) ====
On 17 December 2020, it was announced that [[Wakefield Trinity]] and Huddersfield had agreed to extend Senior's loan period to cover the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55349250|title=Innes Senior: Wakefield Trinity sign Huddersfield Giants winger on loan for 2021 season|work=BBC Sport |accessdate=17 December 2020}}</ref>

In round 20 of the 2023 Super League season, Senior scored four tries for [[Wakefield Trinity]] as they upset Warrington 42-6.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66301927|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity 42-6 Warrington Wolves - Innes Senior claims four tries in victory|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

==== Castleford Tigers (loan) ====
On 11 October 2023, it was announced that Senior would join [[Castleford Tigers]] on a season-long loan for the [[2024 Castleford Tigers season|2024 season]],<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=11 Oct 2023|title=Castleford Tigers and Huddersfield Giants agree swap deal for wing pair|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-huddersfield-giants-agree-swap-deal-for-wing-pair-elliot-wallis-innes-senior}}</ref> in a swap deal with [[Elliot Wallis]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=11 October 2023 |title=Huddersfield Giants swap Innes Senior for Castleford Tigers' Elliot Wallis |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67080853 |access-date=24 March 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He was assigned squad number 5<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=20 January 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers 2024 squad numbers: 11 new signings receive their jerseys, with 31 shirts handed out |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-2024-squad-numbers-11-new-signings-receive-their-jerseys-with-31-shirts-handed-out |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> and scored four tries during pre-season.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=21 January 2024 |title=Four key takeaways after Castleford Tigers and London Broncos can't be split |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/four-key-takeaways-after-castleford-28487138 |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref> He made his Castleford debut in round 1 against [[Wigan Warriors]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=Freeman |first=Jay |date=17 February 2024 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 4-32 Wigan Warriors - Reigning champions begin with hard-earned win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304778 |access-date=25 February 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> and scored his first try in round 3 against [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=1 March 2024 |title=Super League: Warrington Wolves 30-8 Castleford Tigers - Comfortable win for Wire |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68438389 |access-date=2 March 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In round 4, Senior scored two tries against his parent club Huddersfield,<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68501494|title=Super League: Castleford 8-50 Huddersfield - Tigers suffer fourth straight loss to nine-try Giants|website=www.bbc.co.uk|access-date=12 March 2024}}</ref> and in round 7, he scored four tries against [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{cite news |date=5 April 2024 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 36-24 Salford Red Devils - Cas get first win of season |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68723225 |access-date=6 April 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In May, Innes signed a two-year deal to remain with Castleford from the 2025 season alongside his twin brother [[Louis Senior|Louis]], who had also joined Castleford on loan a month prior.<ref name="BBC Permanent deal">{{Cite web |date=22 May 2024 |title=Senior twins signs Castleford contracts |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cn33g4vv8x9o |access-date=18 July 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He finished the season as Castleford's top tryscorer with 17 tries, in addition to playing every minute of every game.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chapman Coombe |first=Louis |date=22 September 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers' eye-catching potential line-up for 2025 if all rumoured targets sign |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-2025-potential-line-up |access-date=24 March 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

=== Castleford Tigers ===
Senior remained at Castleford for the [[2025 Castleford Tigers season|2025 season]] having signed a two-year deal in May 2024.<ref name="BBC Permanent deal" /> He scored his first try of the campaign away at [[Leeds Rhinos]] in round 3 with a 75-metre break down the touchline.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Lanigan |first=Adam |date=2 March 2025 |title=Leeds cruise to victory over struggling Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cn4ymrleyjeo |access-date=1 May 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In round 6 against [[Hull F.C.|Hull]], Senior was controversially sin-binned for adjudged head contact with [[Cade Cust]] and received a two-match ban.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Jackson |first=Will |date=27 March 2025 |title='Disgrace' – Rugby League fans say the same thing after controversial Castleford Tigers v Hull FC decision |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/disgrace-rugby-league-fans-say-the-same-thing-after-controversial-castleford-tigers-v-hull-fc-decision-102960/ |access-date=1 May 2025 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=31 March 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers winger Innes Senior banned and Hull FC players charged following latest disciplinary review |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-winger-innes-senior-banned-and-hull-fc-players-charged-following-latest-disciplinary-review-5059444 |access-date=1 May 2025 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=1 April 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers make appeal decision on Innes Senior ban |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-make-appeal-decision-on-innes-senior-ban/ |access-date=1 May 2025 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> He made his return from suspension against former club [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]], scoring two tries in a man-of-the-match display.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cresswell |first=Matthew |date=26 April 2025 |title=Five-try Castleford thrash dismal Huddersfield |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c5ygw3j9w7vo |access-date=2 May 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=26 April 2025 |title=Castleford Tigers player ratings as Innes Senior haunts former club |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/castleford-tigers-player-ratings-innes-1115998 |access-date=2 May 2025 |website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref>

On 9 October 2025, it was announced that Senior had left [[Castleford Tigers]] one year early ahead of a subsequent transfer to [[Leigh Leopards]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|first=Ben |last=Olawumi|date=9 October 2025|title=Castleford Tigers star secures release ahead of cross-Super League switch|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/innes-senior-secures-castleford-release-ahead-of-cross-super-league-switch|accessdate=9 October 2025}}</ref>

===Leigh Leopards===
On 18 October 2025 it was reported that he had signed a 2-year deal to join [[Leigh Leopards]] for 2026<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=18 Oct 2025|author=Stephen Ibbetson|title=Leigh Leopards sign Castleford Tigers winger Innes Senior|url=https://www.totalrl.com/leigh-leopards-sign-castleford-tigers-winger-innes-senior/}}</ref>
